attack_surface.data_description: Database connection credentials used by Scraper Agent (105)

security_gap.gap_description: If the Scraper Agent's (105) database credentials used for Data Flow 419 also grant permissions for migrations (Doc 12), compromising the agent could lead to broader database compromise than just data writing.

actor_risk.description: The Scraper Agent (105) might possess database credentials with more privileges (e.g., for migrations as per Doc 12) than strictly necessary for routine data writing (Behavior 613).

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromise of the Scraper Agent (105) could allow an attacker to leverage its potentially overprivileged database credentials to modify database schema, delete data (Asset 215 and others), or access other databases if credentials are not scoped properly.

assumptions: The database account used by the Scraper Agent (105) for writing data (Behavior 613, Data Flow 419) also has privileges to 'Manage database migrations (Doc 12)'.
The Scraper Agent (105) itself could be compromised, allowing an attacker to leverage its database credentials.

mitigation.strategy: Apply the principle of least privilege. Use separate, restricted database accounts for routine data writing versus schema migrations. Secure the Scraper Agent (105) and its credentials robustly.

description: If the Scraper Agent (105) is compromised or its database credentials grant excessive permissions (e.g., for managing migrations as per Doc 12, beyond just writing data for Behavior 613), an attacker could gain elevated privileges on the Postgres Database (313), allowing unauthorized data modification, deletion, or schema changes.
